content.problemStatement:
Ada is one of the best hairdressers in ChefLand. She invented a very cool hair style and named it "power tree".

A power tree with degree lll ‚ÄĒ let's denote it by PT(l)PT(l)PT(l) ‚ÄĒ is a directed graph, which can be constructed recursively as follows:


PT(0)PT(0)PT(0) consists of a single node.
For l>0l \gt 0l>0:

Create one node uuu ‚ÄĒ the root of power tree PT(l)PT(l)PT(l).
Construct all power trees with smaller degrees, i.e. PT(0),PT(1),‚Ä¶,PT(l‚ąí1)PT(0), PT(1), \dots, PT(l-1)PT(0),PT(1),‚Ä¶,PT(l‚ąí1).
For each of the tree created in the previous step, add a directed edge from uuu to its root.


Today, Ada is going to cut the hair of some customers. Of course, all of them want to have a power tree hair style!

A customer's uncut hair is represented by a directed graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and exactly one edge between each pair of vertices. Ada should remove some edges (possibly none) from this graph in such a way that the resulting graph is a power tree. Note that she is not allowed to remove vertices!

Can you help Ada find the edges she should remove?
constraints: there is exactly one edge between each pair of vertices
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next (N2)N \choose 2(2N‚Äč) lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv describing a directed edge from vertex uuu to vertex vvv. The edges are numbered 111 through (N2)N \choose 2(2N‚Äč) in this order.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to create a power tree, print a single line containing one integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, print two lines. The first of these lines should contain a single integer eee denoting the number of edges to remove. The second line should contain eee space-separated integers denoting the numbers of the edges to remove.
If there are multiple answers, you may print any one.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Ada should remove edges (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4), (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4) and (3,1)(3, 1)(3,1). The resulting graph is a power tree with degree l=2l=2l=2.
